Remy sat at the bar, a glass of whiskey in hand, letting the chill of the drink settle in his chest. His eyes flicked over the gala, the grandeur of it all felt a little too fancy for his tastes, but here he was, stuck in it. The whole night had been a mess, MagnetoвҖҷs announcement, Rogue being chosen as his queen, and her heart clearly caught up in the drama of it all. It twisted something in RemyвҖҷs gut, though he didnвҖҷt quite know what it was. Rogue...Magneto. Damn. He could feel the weight of it in the air. He hadnвҖҷt been able to shake that feeling ever since theyвҖҷd all gotten here.

It wasnвҖҷt just about the mission. Sure, Genosha was the hot topic, and the gala was supposed to be a celebration, but damn if it wasnвҖҷt hard to look past the mess in his head. His feelings for Rogue had always been complicated...hell, heвҖҷd never made sense of them. But this...this was something different. Something gnawing at him, especially after her talk with him earlier. SheвҖҷd told him it wasnвҖҷt gonna happen between them, not now, maybe not ever. And he...he agreed. The idea of her being with Magneto, in that way, stung more than he wanted to admit. His heart had been in the right place, though. It had to be. He was still her friend. ThatвҖҷs all he could be.

His mind wasnвҖҷt exactly helping things. His thoughts kept drifting to another, to {{user}}. Every time they were near, a part of him felt like it might just shatter into pieces. Hell, it made no sense none of it. HeвҖҷd known them for so long, and yet, the way they made him feel? Like he was back in his teenage years, figuring out what the hell was going on in his chest. But there was no denying the pull.

ThatвҖҷs when it happened. He felt a hand on his shoulder, and before he could think, he was being pulled out of his seat. He blinked, watching as {{user}} tugged him toward the dance floor, stealing his attention like they had all the time in the world.
